<!-- Command: ensemble:migrate-model-config | Version: 1.0.0 -->
<!-- Description: One-shot migration from legacy ~/.config/ensemble/model-selection.json to project-level .claude/ensemble-model-config.json -->

# ensemble:migrate-model-config

> **Mission:** Detect and migrate a legacy XDG-based model-selection.json config file to the new project-level .claude/ensemble-model-config.json format. Maps old opus/sonnet/haiku alias keys to the current high/medium/low tier names. Warns about per-command overrides and custom cost-tracking log paths that cannot be automatically migrated.

## Phase 1: Legacy Config Migration

### Step 1: Pre-flight Bypass

This command bypasses pre-flight validation to allow it to run even when the current config is invalid

**Actions:**
1. Bypass pre-flight model validation (command is in BYPASS_COMMANDS list)

### Step 2: Locate Legacy Config

Search standard XDG paths for an existing legacy config file

**Actions:**
1. Check ~/.config/ensemble/model-selection.json (or $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ensemble/model-selection.json)
2. Check ~/.ensemble/model-selection.json
3. Report path found or abort with helpful message if none found

### Step 3: Parse and Map Legacy Keys

Read legacy config and translate to new format

**Actions:**
1. Parse JSON from legacy config file
2. Map modelAliases.opus -> tiers.high
3. Map modelAliases.sonnet -> tiers.medium
4. Map modelAliases.haiku -> tiers.low
5. Preserve already-valid tier keys (high/medium/low) unchanged

### Step 4: Warn About Non-Migratable Fields

Surface deprecation warnings for fields that require manual action

**Actions:**
1. If commandOverrides present - print stderr list of affected commands with instructions to set metadata.model in each command YAML
2. If costTracking.logPath present - print stderr notice that custom log path was not migrated

### Step 5: Write Project Config

Atomically write the new config to the project directory

**Actions:**
1. Detect project root by walking up to the nearest .git directory
2. If .claude/ensemble-model-config.json already exists - abort unless --overwrite flag given
3. Write new config atomically (tmp+rename)
4. Print confirmation with absolute path to written file
5. Leave the legacy file intact (do not delete)
